story.intro: The bells of Highcrown ring over a starving kingdom. Aspyria is at war, and the King's army is breaking against cursed necromancers from the eastern barrows. The King names you his chosen blade: end the curse, break the dead host, and bring the dawn back to Aspyria.

Necromancer Crown.name: Necromancer Crown
Necromancer Crown.drawback: Full set makes incoming damage 20% higher.

kings_lens.name: King's Lens
kings_lens.description: Level-up choices are more likely to include your favored stat.

royal_drillmark.name: Royal Drillmark
royal_drillmark.description: Normal level-up stat choices are stronger.

saints_refuge.name: Saint's Refuge
saints_refuge.description: Once per run, reduces the price of fleeing.

graveglass_compass.name: Graveglass Compass
graveglass_compass.description: Improves loot odds after necromancer fights.

dialogue.intro: A Grave Thrall drags itself from the mud, still wearing a soldier's ring.
dialogue.attack: The Grave Thrall claws at your shield.
dialogue.defense: The Grave Thrall folds under your strike and rises again.
dialogue.player_victory: The Grave Thrall falls quiet.
dialogue.player_loss: The Grave Thrall kneels over you, waiting for the curse to finish its work.

dialogue.intro: A Bone Pike Acolyte points a white spear at your heart.
dialogue.attack: The acolyte thrusts with both hands.
dialogue.defense: The acolyte hides behind a ribbed ward.
dialogue.player_victory: The acolyte's spear snaps.
dialogue.player_loss: The acolyte whispers your name into the dirt.

dialogue.intro: An Ash-Robed Hexer smiles through a veil of corpse-smoke.
dialogue.attack: The hexer throws a black charm.
dialogue.defense: Ash swallows your blade's path.
dialogue.player_victory: The hexer's charms scatter like dead moths.
dialogue.player_loss: The hexer marks your armor for the carrion priests.

dialogue.intro: The Barrow Knight rises beneath a cracked royal banner.
dialogue.boss_intro: The Barrow Knight speaks through rusted teeth: 'The King sends children now?'
dialogue.attack: The Barrow Knight carves a heavy arc.
dialogue.defense: Old plate turns your edge aside.
dialogue.player_victory: The Barrow Knight sinks to one knee and remembers the sun.
dialogue.boss_victory: The King's scouts cheer from the hill. The western road is open.
dialogue.player_loss: The Barrow Knight plants your banner in the grave-soil.

dialogue.intro: A Crypt Archer draws a bow strung with hair.
dialogue.attack: A black-fletched arrow cuts the air.
dialogue.defense: The archer slips behind a broken angel statue.
dialogue.player_victory: The bowstring breaks with a shriek.
dialogue.player_loss: The archer lowers the bow only when you stop moving.

dialogue.intro: A Bell-Tower Revenant tolls a handbell with no clapper.
dialogue.attack: The revenant's bell cracks against your helm.
dialogue.defense: A bell note bends your strike away.
dialogue.player_victory: The bell falls silent.
dialogue.player_loss: The revenant tolls once for your death.

dialogue.intro: A Moon-Bitten Scout circles you with a stolen cavalry knife.
dialogue.attack: The scout darts in low.
dialogue.defense: The scout rolls beneath your guard.
dialogue.player_victory: The scout collapses under the moon's pale stare.
dialogue.player_loss: The scout whistles for the corpse wagons.

dialogue.intro: The Choirmaster of Bones lifts a baton made from a saint's finger.
dialogue.boss_intro: 'Sing with us,' the Choirmaster says, and the dead open their mouths.
dialogue.attack: A chorus of skulls hammers your thoughts.
dialogue.defense: The Choirmaster steps through a curtain of bones.
dialogue.player_victory: The dead choir breaks into dust.
dialogue.boss_victory: Highcrown's priests reclaim the chapel bells.
dialogue.player_loss: Your voice joins the choir.

dialogue.intro: A Rot-Hound Pack bursts from the corpse grass.
dialogue.attack: The pack tears at you from three sides.
dialogue.defense: The hounds scatter and reform.
dialogue.player_victory: The last hound burns away in grey sparks.
dialogue.player_loss: The pack drags you toward the barrows.

dialogue.intro: A Mirror Wight wears your face badly.
dialogue.attack: The wight mimics your killing stance.
dialogue.defense: The wight fractures into false reflections.
dialogue.player_victory: The false face shatters.
dialogue.player_loss: The wight walks away wearing your courage.

dialogue.intro: A Mawplate Butcher drags a cleaver through sparks.
dialogue.attack: The cleaver bites into your guard.
dialogue.defense: The butcher laughs behind a jawbone visor.
dialogue.player_victory: The butcher's cleaver sinks into the dirt.
dialogue.player_loss: The butcher measures you for the feast pits.

dialogue.intro: The Hollow Duke bows with courtly grace.
dialogue.boss_intro: 'Tell my cousin the King I still hold court,' the Hollow Duke says.
dialogue.attack: The Duke's paired blades flash twice.
dialogue.defense: A dead courtier takes the blow for him.
dialogue.player_victory: The Hollow Duke's crown rolls into the dust.
dialogue.boss_victory: The King's army retakes the old court road.
dialogue.player_loss: The Duke names you jester of his dead court.

dialogue.intro: A Plague Ledger Priest writes your debts in red ink.
dialogue.attack: The priest snaps the ledger shut like a trap.
dialogue.defense: Your strike vanishes into a page of names.
dialogue.player_victory: The ledger burns page by page.
dialogue.player_loss: The priest adds your breath to the debt.

dialogue.intro: A Grave Ox lowers horns wrapped in funeral chains.
dialogue.attack: The ox charges like a falling wall.
dialogue.defense: The chains catch your weapon.
dialogue.player_victory: The beast crashes down and does not rise.
dialogue.player_loss: The ox tramples the King's seal into mud.

dialogue.intro: The Funeral Pyre Choir sings in burning harmony.
dialogue.attack: Flame and song lash your armor.
dialogue.defense: Their hymn swallows the blow.
dialogue.player_victory: The last note dies in ash.
dialogue.player_loss: They sing you into the pyre.

dialogue.intro: The Cinder Lich steps from a furnace of bones.
dialogue.boss_intro: 'Aspyria is already ash,' the Cinder Lich says. 'I merely teach it to kneel.'
dialogue.attack: Cinder magic tears across your armor.
dialogue.defense: The lich becomes a column of sparks.
dialogue.player_victory: The Cinder Lich breaks apart in royal sunlight.
dialogue.boss_victory: The foundries of Aspyria flare gold again.
dialogue.player_loss: The lich brands your name into black iron.

dialogue.intro: A Black Cache Angel unfolds wings of locked coffins.
dialogue.attack: A coffin-wing strikes like judgment.
dialogue.defense: The angel seals itself behind a black lock.
dialogue.player_victory: The locks open and release only dust.
dialogue.player_loss: The angel files your soul away.

dialogue.intro: A Root Psalmist rises with thorns through its throat.
dialogue.attack: Root and hymn lash together.
dialogue.defense: The psalmist sinks into the root web.
dialogue.player_victory: The hymn curdles into silence.
dialogue.player_loss: Roots lace around your wrists.

dialogue.intro: The Crowned Null arrives as an absence shaped like royalty.
dialogue.attack: Nothingness strikes through the gap in your guard.
dialogue.defense: Your weapon passes through a crown of shadow.
dialogue.player_victory: The empty crown cracks.
dialogue.player_loss: The Null removes your name from the world.

dialogue.intro: Dread Necromancer Veyr waits beneath a sky of circling dead.
dialogue.boss_intro: 'The King gave you a sword,' Veyr says. 'I gave his dead soldiers purpose.'
dialogue.attack: Veyr tears a curse through the battlefield.
dialogue.defense: A wall of royal corpses takes your blow.
dialogue.player_victory: Veyr's crown of bones breaks, and dawn spills over Aspyria.
dialogue.boss_victory: The curse recoils. Aspyria breathes again, though darker roads remain.
dialogue.player_loss: Veyr raises your shadow and sends it against Highcrown.

story.enter: The war begins in the trampled fields below Highcrown.
Broken bells hang over a chapel where the dead still sing.
The old road to the eastern court is lined with kneeling statues.
The foundries burn with corpse-fire and stolen royal iron.
At the edge of Aspyria, Veyr's citadel claws at the dawn.
